metadata.author: svc.cv-securityiq@commvault.com
metadata.comments: Solution template for Commvault Security IQ

metadata.description: Not used, but needed to pass arm-ttk test `Location-Should-Not-Be-Hardcoded`.  We instead use the `workspace-location` which is derived from the LA workspace
[concat('Region to deploy solution resources -- separate from location selection',parameters('location'))]
Workspace name for Log Analytics where Microsoft Sentinel is setup
resource group name where Microsoft Sentinel is setup
subscription id where Microsoft Sentinel is setup

properties.description: CommvaultSecurityIQ_Alert_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.0.5
This query identifies Alerts from Commvault Cloud.
properties.query:
CommvaultAlertsCCF_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(5m)
| where isnotnull(AnomalyType) and AnomalyType > 0
| take 1000

alertDetailsOverride.alertDescriptionFormat: Anomaly detected for Commvault client {{ClientName}} (ID: {{ClientId}}) at {{Location}}. AnomalyType flag: {{AnomalyType}}. File activity — Created: {{CreateCount}}, Deleted: {{DeleteCount}}, Modified: {{ModCount}}, Renamed: {{RenameCount}}, Infected: {{InfectedFilesCount}}.
alertDetailsOverride.alertDisplayNameFormat: Commvault Cloud Anomaly detected for client: {{ClientName}}

metadata.title: Commvault Disable Data Aging Logic App Playbook
metadata.description: This Logic App executes when called upon by an Automation Rule. Accessing the KeyVault to retrieve various credentials, it executes a specific runbook depending on the use case.
prerequisites: 1. Administrative access to your Commvault/Metallic environment.
2. Administrative access to your Azure Resource Group and Subscription.
3. A Microsoft Sentinel instance in the aforementioned Azure Resource Group.

4. A Keyvault and an Automation Account configured as mentioned in the documentation here :- (https://github.com/Cv-securityIQ/Azure-Integration/blob/master/Solutions/Commvault%20Security%20IQ/README.md)
postDeployment: 1. Steps to follow the instructions are mentioned here :- (https://github.com/Cv-securityIQ/Azure-Integration/blob/master/Solutions/Commvault%20Security%20IQ/README.md)
2. Give the required permissions to the logic app to get the secrets from the keyvault.

properties.displayName: Commvault Security IQ (via Codeless Connector Framework)

connectorUiConfig.title: Commvault Security IQ (via Codeless Connector Framework)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The Commvault Security IQ data connector ingests threat indicator anomaly events from the Commvault `/Client/Anomaly` API into Microsoft Sentinel. Each record in `CommvaultAlertsCCF_CL` corresponds to one entry in the `anomalyClients` array and captures fields such as `AnomalyType` (a bitfield — 64=Ransomware, 8192=VSA Attack), file operation counts (`CreateCount`, `DeleteCount`, `ModCount`, `RenameCount`, `InfectedFilesCount`), data sizes (`ApplicationSizeMB`, `BackupSizeMB`), and client details (`ClientName`, `ClientId`). The connector requires a Commvault Base URL and a valid QSDK Access Token.

sampleQueries.query:
CommvaultAlertsCCF_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| project TimeGenerated, AnomalyType, ClientName, ClientId, CreateCount, DeleteCount, ModCount, RenameCount, InfectedFilesCount, Location
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
| limit 10

CommvaultAlertsCCF_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where binary_and(AnomalyType, 64) == 64 or binary_and(AnomalyType, 8192) == 8192
| project TimeGenerated, ClientName, AnomalyType, InfectedFilesCount, Location
| sort by TimeGenerated desc

sampleQueries.description: Clients with the most anomaly events in the last 7 days
sampleQueries.query:
CommvaultAlertsCCF_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| summarize EventCount = count() by ClientName
| sort by EventCount desc
| limit 20

dataTypes.name: CommvaultAlertsCCF_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
CommvaultAlertsCCF_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(12h)
| summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
| where isnotempty(Time)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and Write permissions are required.

customs.name: Commvault API Credentials
customs.description: A Commvault environment Base URL and a valid QSDK Access Token are required to authorize API requests.

instructionSteps.title: 1. Connector Management
instructionSteps.description: Manage your Commvault Security IQ connector instances. Each instance connects to one Commvault environment.

parameters.content:
## Commvault Security IQ Connector Instances

This connector polls the Commvault `/Client/Anomaly` API every 30 minutes and ingests threat anomaly events into the `CommvaultAlertsCCF_CL` table.

## API Configuration

Provide your Commvault environment endpoint URL and QSDK Access Token.

parameters.text: Enter the base URL of your Commvault Command Center (e.g., `https://example.commvault.com`). Do **not** include `/Client/Anomaly` — the connector appends this automatically.

parameters.label: QSDK Access Token
parameters.placeholder: Enter your Commvault QSDK Access Token
parameters.name: qsdkToken

parameters.text: The QSDK Token is sent as a `Bearer` token in the `Authorization` header. Generate this token from the Commvault Command Center under **Security > API Tokens**.
**Data Collection Schedule**: The connector polls for new anomaly events every 30 minutes.

columns.name: refTime
columns.description: Epoch timestamp (seconds) of the anomaly event from Commvault.

columns.name: anomalyType
columns.description: Bitfield integer identifying the anomaly type(s). Bit 64=Ransomware, Bit 8192=VSA Attack.

columns.name: client
columns.description: Nested object containing clientName and clientId.

dataFlows.transformKql: source | extend TimeGenerated = iff(isnull(refTime), now(), unixtime_seconds_todatetime(tolong(refTime))) , RefTime = tolong(refTime) , AnomalyType = toint(anomalyType) , CreateCount = toint(createCount) , DeleteCount = toint(deleteCount) , ModCount = toint(modCount) , RenameCount = toint(renameCount) , InfectedFilesCount = toint(infectedFilesCount) , ApplicationSizeMB = todouble(applicationSizeMB) , BackupSizeMB = todouble(backupSizeMB) , Location = tostring(location) , ClientName = tostring(parse_json(client).clientName) , ClientId = toint(parse_json(client).clientId) | project TimeGenerated , RefTime , AnomalyType , CreateCount , DeleteCount , ModCount , RenameCount , InfectedFilesCount , ApplicationSizeMB , BackupSizeMB , Location , ClientName , ClientId

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Timestamp derived from the anomaly refTime field.

columns.name: RefTime
columns.description: Raw epoch timestamp (seconds) from the Commvault anomaly event.

columns.name: AnomalyType
columns.description: Bitfield integer representing the type(s) of anomaly detected. Common bits: 64=Ransomware, 8192=VSA Attack.

columns.name: CreateCount
columns.description: Number of files created during the anomaly window.

columns.name: DeleteCount
columns.description: Number of files deleted during the anomaly window.

columns.name: ModCount
columns.description: Number of files modified during the anomaly window.

columns.name: RenameCount
columns.description: Number of files renamed during the anomaly window.

columns.name: InfectedFilesCount
columns.description: Number of files identified as infected.

columns.name: ApplicationSizeMB
columns.description: Application data size in megabytes at time of anomaly.

columns.name: BackupSizeMB
columns.description: Backup data size in megabytes at time of anomaly.

columns.name: Location
columns.description: Location or region associated with the anomaly.

columns.name: ClientName
columns.description: Name of the Commvault client (endpoint) where the anomaly was detected.

columns.name: ClientId
columns.description: Numeric identifier of the Commvault client.

properties.displayName: Commvault Security IQ (via Codeless Connector Framework)

connectorDefinitionName.defaultValue: Commvault Security IQ (via Codeless Connector Framework)

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

This Microsoft Sentinel integration enables Commvault users to ingest alerts and other data into their Microsoft Sentinel instance. With Analytic Rules, Microsoft Sentinel can automatically create Microsoft Sentinel incidents.

Data Connectors: 1, Analytic Rules: 1, Playbooks: 3

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions
